sections.heading: (1) These Regulations may be cited as the Data Protection
sections.title: Regulation citation
sections.summary: These Regulations are formally named the Data Protection (Registration of Data Controllers and Data Processors) Regulations, 2021. They establish the legal framework for data controller and processor registration in Kenya under the Data Protection Act.

requirements.text: These Regulations may be cited as the Data Protection (Registration of Data Controllers and Data Processors) Regulations, 2021

sections.source_quote: These Regulations may be cited as the Data Protection (Registration of Data Controllers and Data Processors) Regulations, 2021

sections.heading: In these Regulations, unless the context otherwise requires
sections.title: Definitions of establishment documents
sections.summary: This section defines 'establishment' as including statutory instruments, registration certificates, trust deeds, and other instruments that establish a body. It clarifies the scope of documents required for registration purposes.

requirements.text: establishment means (a) a Statute, Charter or statutory instrument in which a body is established; (b) registration certificate; (c) trust deeds in which a trust has been established; and (d) other instruments by which a body is established including its governing and administrative structure

sections.source_quote: establishment means (a) a Statute, Charter or statutory instrument in which a body is established; (b) registration certificate; (c) trust deeds in which a trust has been established; and (d) other instruments by which a body is established including its governing and administrative structure

sections.heading: (1) These Regulations provide for the procedure for
sections.title: Registration procedure scope
sections.summary: These Regulations outline the registration procedure for data controllers and processors as per the Act. They explicitly exclude civil registration entities from this registration requirement, which are governed by separate Data Protection (Civil Registration) Regulations.

requirements.text: These Regulations provide for the procedure for registration of data controllers and data processors as provided under section 18 of the Act
These Regulations shall not apply to civil registration entities specified under the Data Protection (Civil Registration) Regulations

sections.source_quote: These Regulations provide for the procedure for registration of data controllers and data processors as provided under section 18 of the Act. These Regulations shall not apply to civil registration entities specified under the Data Protection (Civil Registration) Regulations

sections.heading: (1) Subject to regulation 13 (1), every data controller and data
sections.title: Registration requirements for data controllers and processors
sections.summary: Data controllers and processors must register with the Data Protection Authority. A data controller determines data processing purposes, while a data processor must have a contractual relationship with the controller and no decision-making power over processing purposes. A controller may apply for dual registration, and a processor acting outside instructions becomes a controller for that activity.

requirements.text: Subject to regulation 13 (1), every data controller and data processor shall be required to register in accordance with the provisions of the Act and these Regulations
For purposes of registration, a person shall register as a data controller where the person determines the purpose and means for processing personal data

For purposes of registration, a person shall register as a data processor where the person processes personal data on behalf of the data controller but excludes employees of the data controller and has (i) a contractual relationship with the data controller; and (ii) no decision making power on the purpose and means of processing personal data

Despite sub-regulation (2) (a), a data controller may apply for registration as both a data controller and a data processor with regards to any processing operations and shall be required to pay the requisite fees applicable for both a data controller and a data processor thereto

Despite sub-regulation (2) (b), where a data processor processes personal data other than as instructed by the data controller, the data processor shall be considered to be a data controller in respect of that processing activity, for purposes of assessing liability

sections.source_quote: Subject to regulation 13 (1), every data controller and data processor shall be required to register in accordance with the provisions of the Act and these Regulations. For purposes of registration, a person shall register as a data controller, where the person determines the purpose and means for processing personal data; or a data processor, where the person processes personal data on behalf of the data controller but excludes employees of the data controller and has (i) a contractual relationship with the data controller; and (ii) no decision making power on the purpose and means of processing personal data.

sections.heading: (1) An application for registration of a data controller or data
sections.title: Application for registration requirements
sections.summary: Applications for registration must be submitted in Form DPR1 and include registration fees, establishment documents, data controller/processor particulars, and descriptions of processing purposes and data categories. This ensures comprehensive information is provided for regulatory review.

requirements.text: An application for registration of a data controller or data processor shall be in Form DPR1 set out in the First Schedule
An application for registration shall be accompanied by the registration fees specified in the Second Schedule
An application for registration shall be accompanied by a copy of the establishment documents
An application for registration shall be accompanied by particulars of the data controllers or data processors including name and contact details
An application for registration shall be accompanied by a description of the purpose for which personal data is processed
An application for registration shall be accompanied by a description of categories of personal data being processed

sections.source_quote: An application for registration of a data controller or data processor shall be in Form DPR1 set out in the First Schedule; and be accompanied by the registration fees specified in the Second Schedule. An application for registration under sub-regulation (1) shall be accompanied by (a) a copy of the establishment documents; (b) particulars of the data controllers or data processors including name and contact details; (c) a description of the purpose for which personal data is processed; and (d) a description of categories of personal data being processed.

sections.heading: (1) A state department or county department shall register and
sections.title: Public entities registration
sections.summary: State and county departments must register and pay fees for public entities that operate within their departments, are funded from the Consolidated Fund, and provide public services. State and county corporations must also register as data controllers or processors for their processing activities.

requirements.text: A state department or county department shall register and pay the fees on behalf of their respective entities

The entities referred to under sub-regulation (1) shall be the public entities at national or county government which (a) operates within a state department or county department; (b) is wholly funded from the Consolidated Fund; and (c) provides a public service
The fees paid by the state department or county department under sub-regulation (1) shall cater for the specified entities registered under the concerned state department or county department

Despite this regulation, a State Corporation or a County Corporation shall be required to register as a data controller or a data processor in respect of their processing activity, in the manner specified under these Regulations

sections.source_quote: A state department or county department shall register and pay the fees on behalf of their respective entities. The entities referred to under sub-regulation (1) shall be the public entities at national or county government which operates within a state department or county department; is wholly funded from the Consolidated Fund; and provides a public service. The fees paid by the state department or county department under sub-regulation (1) shall cater for the specified entities registered under the concerned state department or county department. Despite this regulation, a State Corporation or a County Corporation shall be required to register as a data controller or a data processor in respect of their processing activity, in the manner specified under these Regulations.

sections.heading: Where the Data Commissioner is satisfied that the applicant
sections.title: Registration approval and certificate issuance
sections.summary: The Data Commissioner must issue a certificate of registration for the duration specified under regulation 9 and enter the applicant's particulars in the register within fourteen days if satisfied with the application. This applies to data controllers and data processors seeking registration under the Data Protection Regulations.

requirements.text: Issue certificate of registration for duration specified under regulation 9
Enter applicant's particulars in the register

sections.source_quote: Where the Data Commissioner is satisfied that the applicant fulfills the requirements for registration under these Regulations, the Data Commissioner shall, within fourteen days (a) issue the applicant with a certificate of registration for the duration specified under regulation 9; and (b) enter the particulars of the successful applicant in the register.

sections.heading: A certificate of registration issued under regulation 8 (a) shall
sections.title: Certificate validity period
sections.summary: A certificate of registration issued under regulation 8(a) remains valid for twenty-four months from the date of issuance. This applies to data controllers and data processors registered under the Data Protection Regulations.

requirements.text: Certificate of registration is valid for twenty-four months from date of issuance

sections.source_quote: A certificate of registration issued under regulation 8 (a) shall be valid for a period of twenty-four months from the date of issuance.

sections.heading: (1) Where the Data Commissioner declines to approve an
sections.title: Registration refusal procedures
sections.summary: The Data Commissioner must notify applicants in writing of refusal within twenty-one days and provide reasons for refusal. Refusal may occur if application particulars are insufficient, appropriate privacy safeguards are not provided, or the applicant violates the Act or Regulations. Applicants may reapply after complying with refusal requirements.

requirements.text: Notify applicant in writing of refusal within twenty-one days
Provide reasons for refusal
Refusal may occur if application particulars are insufficient
Refusal may occur if appropriate privacy safeguards are not provided
Refusal may occur if data controller or processor violates Act or Regulations
Applicant may reapply after complying with refusal requirements

sections.source_quote: (1) Where the Data Commissioner declines to approve an application for registration, the Data Commissioner shall within twenty-one days from the date of such decision (a) notify, in writing, the applicant of the refusal; and (b) provide reasons for such refusal.

sections.heading: (1) Pursuant to section 20 of the Act, a registered data
sections.title: Registration renewal process
sections.summary: Registered data controllers or processors must apply for renewal of registration after their certificate expires. Renewal applications must be made in Form PR 2 and accompanied by the appropriate renewal fee. The Data Commissioner may require verification if renewal is for distinct purposes or data categories.

requirements.text: Apply for renewal of registration after certificate expiry
Make renewal application in Form PR 2
Accompany renewal application with appropriate renewal fee
Data Commissioner may require verification if renewal is for distinct purposes or data categories

sections.source_quote: Pursuant to section 20 of the Act, a registered data controller or data processor shall apply for a renewal of registration as a data controller or data processor, after the expiry of the certificate of registration.

sections.heading: (1) Where the Data Commissioner declines to renew an
sections.title: Genetic Data Processing Registration
sections.summary: This section specifies that businesses processing genetic data must register with the Data Commissioner as data controllers or processors. It serves as a threshold for mandatory registration under the Data Protection Act.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires genetic data processing activities to be registered, which impacts DTIAs by necessitating specific safeguards for highly sensitive data transfers.

requirements.text: Businesses processing genetic data must register with Data Commissioner

sections.heading: (1) For purposes of this regulation
sections.title: Exemptions from mandatory registration
sections.summary: Data controllers or processors with annual turnover below five million shillings and fewer than ten employees are exempt from mandatory registration. However, exemptions do not apply if processing personal data for purposes specified in the Third Schedule, and exempt entities must comply with Part IV and Part VI of the Act.

requirements.text: Exempt from mandatory registration if annual turnover below five million shillings and fewer than ten employees
Exemptions do not apply if processing personal data for purposes specified in Third Schedule
Exempt entities must comply with Part IV and Part VI of the Act

sections.source_quote: A data controller or data processor is exempt from mandatory registration under these Regulations where the data controller or data processor (a) has an annual turnover of below five million shillings or annual revenue of below five million shillings; and (b) has less than ten employees.

sections.heading: (1) Subject to section 21 of the Act, the Data Commissioner
sections.title: Data Controller and Processor Registration Register Maintenance
sections.summary: The Data Commissioner must maintain an up-to-date register containing registered data controllers and processors, their categories of personal data processed, business addresses, data protection officer details, and other relevant particulars. The register must be updated within 14 days of any changes to registration details by the data controller or processor. The Data Commissioner publishes a list of registered entities on the official website every 30 days.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires maintaining a real-time register of data controllers and processors with their data processing activities, which impacts cross-border data transfers by ensuring entities are properly registered before handling personal data internationally.

requirements.text: Data Commissioner shall keep and maintain an up-to-date register containing names and particulars of registered data controllers and data processors
Register must include categories of personal data processed by data controllers and processors
Register must contain addresses of principal business locations of data controllers and processors
Register must include details of data protection officers where applicable
Register must include any other relevant particulars
Data Commissioner must publish a list of registered data controllers or processors on official website every 30 days

sections.source_quote: 14. (1) Subject to section 21 of the Act, the Data Commissioner shall keep and maintain an up to date register which shall contain (a) the names and particulars of registered data controllers and data processors; (b) categories of personal data being processed by the data controllers and data processors; (c) the address of the principal places of business of the data controllers and data processors; (d) where applicable, details of data protection officers; and (e) any other relevant particular. (2) The Office shall, once every thirty days, publish on the official website a list of registered data controllers or data processors.

sections.heading: (1) Subject to section 19(2) of the Act, a data controller or
sections.title: Registration Details Notification Requirements
sections.summary: Data controllers and processors must notify the Data Commissioner in writing within 14 days of any changes to their registration particulars. The Data Commissioner must update the register upon receiving such notifications and may request additional documentation before making changes. Failure to comply constitutes an offence with penalties under section 73 of the Act.
sections.dtia_summary: Mandates timely notification of registration changes, which is critical for DTIAs to ensure current registration status before cross-border data transfers.

requirements.text: Data controller or processor must notify Data Commissioner in writing within 14 days of any changes to registration particulars
Data Commissioner must update register after receiving notification
Data Commissioner may request necessary documents or proof before updating register
Failure to comply with this regulation constitutes an offence with penalties under section 73 of the Act

sections.source_quote: 15. (1) Subject to section 19(2) of the Act, a data controller or data processor shall, within fourteen days of the occurrence of any changes in the particulars of a data controller or a data processor, notify the Data Commissioner in writing. (2) The Data Commissioner shall, on receiving the notification make the necessary changes on the register, where necessary. (3) The Data Commissioner may prior to making any change on the register, request for any necessary documents or proof thereof. (4) A data controller or data processor who contravenes this regulation commits an offence and shall, on conviction, be liable to the penalty specified under section 73 of the Act.

sections.heading: (1) Subject to section 22 of the Act, the Data Commissioner
sections.title: Registration Certificate Cancellation or Variation
sections.summary: The Data Commissioner may cancel or vary registration conditions for data controllers or processors if they provide false information, willfully or negligently fail to comply with the Act and regulations, or apply for cancellation/variation. The Commissioner must follow the Fair Administrative Actions Act, 2015 when making such decisions.
sections.dtia_summary: Provides mechanisms for revoking registration where data controllers/processors fail to meet compliance requirements, which affects DTIAs by requiring thorough vetting of entities before cross-border data transfers.

requirements.text: Data Commissioner may cancel certificate of registration or vary conditions for registration
Cancellation or variation may occur if data controller or processor applies for cancellation or variation
Cancellation or variation may occur if Data Commissioner establishes false or misleading information was provided
Cancellation or variation may occur if data controller or processor willfully or negligently fails to comply with Act and regulations
Data Commissioner must follow Fair Administrative Actions Act, 2015 when cancelling or varying registration

sections.source_quote: 16. (1) Subject to section 22 of the Act, the Data Commissioner may cancel a certificate of registration or vary the conditions for registration, where (a) the data controller or data processor applies for cancellation or variation; (b) the Data Commissioner establishes that the data controller or data processor provided false or misleading information in relation to any registration particulars; or (c) the data controller or data processor willfully or negligently, fails to comply with provisions of the Act and any Regulations made thereunder. (2) The Data Commissioner shall, before cancelling or varying the conditions of registration, be guided by the provisions of the Fair Administrative Actions Act, 2015.

sections.heading: A data controller or a data processor who
sections.title: Registration Offences
sections.summary: Data controllers or processors who fail to register personal data processing activities, provide false information during registration, or fail to renew their certificate of registration after expiry commit offences under this regulation. Penalties are specified in section 73 of the Act.
sections.dtia_summary: Defines specific offences related to registration compliance, which are critical for DTIAs to assess whether entities meet registration requirements before cross-border data transfers.

requirements.text: Data controller or processor who processes personal data without registering commits an offence
Data controller or processor who provides false or misleading information for registration commits an offence
Data controller or processor who fails to renew certificate of registration and continues processing after expiry commits an offence
Offenders face penalties specified under section 73 of the Act

sections.source_quote: 18. A data controller or a data processor who (a) processes personal data without registering in accordance with these Regulations; (b) provides false or misleading information for the purpose of registration; or (c) fails to renew a certificate of registration and continues to process personal data after the expiry of the certificate, commits an offence and shall, upon conviction, be liable to penalty specified under section 73 of the Act.

sections.heading: Businesses that process genetic data.
sections.title: Genetic Data Processing Registration
sections.summary: This section specifies that businesses processing genetic data must register with the Data Commissioner as data controllers or processors. It serves as a threshold for mandatory registration under the Data Protection Act.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires genetic data processing activities to be registered, which impacts DTIAs by necessitating specific safeguards for highly sensitive data transfers.

requirements.text: Businesses processing genetic data must register with Data Commissioner
